overview: This is a Devil's Matchstick (Pilophorus acicularis), a type of lichen that thrives on rocks in cool, moist forests. It is identified by its upright, pale green stalks, which typically measure 10–30 mm tall. Each stalk terminates in a small, shiny black structure called an apothecium, which is used for reproduction.
